title: Craftable Enchanted Books
description: Say goodbye to RNG. Craft your enchanted books with progression-based recipes.
body:
Turn enchanting into a structured crafting system.

**Craftable Enchanted Books** introduces a brand new block: the **Enchanted Book Station**, a custom workstation that allows players to craft enchanted books through structured recipes instead of relying only on RNG.

The system works with both Vanilla enchantments and enchantments added by other mods.

No villagers.  
No enchantment table randomness.  
Pure progression-based crafting.

# Enchanted Book Station

![Image of Enchanted Book Station](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6326600b3c0bb103c70b934a0482d961fb6e0598.png)
![Image of Gui of Enchanted Book Station](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9d3dbfb431cc2d8689672a9fa3d32a54fe7600cd.png)

The **Enchanted Book Station** is a custom block with a dedicated GUI similar to the vanilla crafting table.

Through this interface, you can craft enchanted books using a fixed template combined with specific materials depending on:

- The enchantment type
- The desired level
  
## Enchanted Book Station Crafting Recipe
![Crafting recipe of Enchanted Book Station](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/88985b4c0ac2dea3e29ad788eeb2fd799494e1eb.png)

# How Crafting Works

All enchanted book recipes follow a **fixed core template**:

- 1 Book
- 2 Lapis Lazuli
- 2 Amethyst Shards

Then you add:

- An item that represents the enchantment
- A material that determines the enchantment level
  
## Example of crafting
![Crafting recipe of Feather Falling 1](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/4441afff45125f681166f8164b17e2e959a9c3ef.png)

All crafting recipes can be found in the Images section. (If the name of the enchanted book is not shown in the image then it is shown in the description and/or title of the image)
Each enchantment has one recipe shown, without listing all levels.  
Bane of Arthropods is included as an example to demonstrate the different crafting levels.

# Multiple Enchantments per Recipe

Some enchantments from different mods may share the same recipe.

To avoid compatibility issues, the GUI allows you to cycle between multiple possible results using the arrow buttons. 

This lets you select the enchanted book you want when multiple enchantments match the same ingredients.

As you can see in the example below, with the same recipe it is possible to obtain Curse Of Binding from Vanilla or Cobweb from Enchantments Expansion.

![Recipe for Cobweb from Enchantments Expansion](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8a44ae1145da94676bf1d497d9ddef5362496980.png)![Reciep for Curse of Binding from Minecraft Vanilla](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ddf7d1967f09fffb89a528991e8d41adb06dfd44.png)

# Enchantment Source Information

To help you find the right **Enchanted Book**, **Enchanted books** now include additional information in their tooltip:

Vanilla enchantments

- The book will display that the enchantment comes from Vanilla Minecraft

Modded enchantments

- The tooltip displays:
  - Mod name
  - Mod author
  - Enchantment description

This helps players easily identify where an enchantment comes from.

# Level Progression System

The material used defines the level of the enchantment:

- Copper → Lv. 1
- Gold → Lv. 2
- Diamond → Lv. 3
- Emerald → Lv. 4
- Netherite Ingot → Lv. 5

# Mod Compatibility

Craftable Enchanted Books is designed to work with enchantments from other mods.

Currently supported:

- [Enchantments Expansion](https://modrinth.com/datapack/enchantments-expansion)

More mod compatibility will be added in future updates.

If you would like support for enchantments from a specific mod, feel free to suggest it in the comments or by message.
